title: lasthenia volkmanni
intro: Lasthenia volkmanni, also known as Volkman's goldfields or yellow-rayed sunflower, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antifungal activities. Lasthenia volkmanni can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
